binaries.title: irenum.sys (IR Bus Enumerator) - No security-relevant change (redundant zero-init of registry-path pool buffer)

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry
key_changes: Added memset(Path, 0, NumberOfBytes) at 0x1C00080A4 to zero the full ExAllocatePoolWithTag allocation before the registry path is copied in

In the unpatched build the same buffer is already fully written for the inputs that occur: memmove writes Length bytes and the wide null-terminator write stores a 2-byte zero at offset 2*(Length/2), which for even Length equals Length and covers the remaining [Length, Length+2) bytes
RegistryPath->Length is a UNICODE_STRING byte count supplied by the OS and is always even, so no uninitialized tail bytes exist on the path that runs
The buffer (global P at 0x1C0003028) is consumed only as a null-terminated absolute registry path by RtlQueryRegistryValues and is never returned to user mode
changed_functions.security_notes: DriverEntry (0x1C0008008) is the driver initialization routine, invoked once at load by the entry stub GsDriverEntry (0x1C0008260). It receives the driver RegistryPath (UNICODE_STRING*) in rdx, rejects Length >= 0xFFFD, allocates NumberOfBytes = Length + 2 bytes of PagedPool via ExAllocatePoolWithTag (tag 'IrEn' = 0x6E457249), stores the pointer in global P (0x1C0003028) and the size in global word_1C0003022, then copies Length bytes with memmove and writes a wide null terminator. The patched build adds memset(Path, 0, NumberOfBytes) before the copy. This is redundant defensive hardening, not a security fix: for the even RegistryPath->Length that always occurs, the copy plus the wide null-terminator (mov [rdi+rax*2], r14w with rax = Length>>1) together initialize the entire Length+2 allocation, so no uninitialized bytes are present in the unpatched build. The buffer is used only as the null-terminated absolute path for RtlQueryRegistryValues (reading BreakOnEntry, DebugFlags, EnumStaticDevice) and is never surfaced to user mode. DriverEntry runs once at PASSIVE_LEVEL in system-thread context and is not reachable from an untrusted request surface.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
--- UNPATCHED DriverEntry (0x1C0008084-0x1C00080BF)
0x1C0008084: mov  cs:P, rax                 ; global P = Path
0x1C000808B: mov  rdi, rax                   ; rdi = Path
0x1C000808E: test rax, rax
0x1C0008091: jz   loc_1C0008231             ; null check
0x1C0008097: movzx r8d, word ptr [rsi]      ; Size = RegistryPath->Length
0x1C000809B: mov  rcx, rax                   ; dst = Path
0x1C000809E: mov  rdx, [rsi+8]              ; src = RegistryPath->Buffer
0x1C00080A2: call memmove                    ; copy Length bytes
0x1C00080A7: movzx eax, word ptr [rsi]       ; eax = Length
0x1C00080AF: shr  rax, 1                      ; rax = Length/2
0x1C00080BA: mov  [rdi+rax*2], r14w          ; Path[2*(Length/2)] = 0 (wide null-term)
0x1C00080BF: call memset                     ; memset(&QueryTable, 0, 0xE0) -- local table, not Path

+++ PATCHED DriverEntry (0x1C0008084-0x1C00080D1)
0x1C0008084: mov  cs:P, rax                 ; global P = Path
0x1C000808B: mov  rdi, rax                   ; rdi = Path
0x1C000808E: test rax, rax
0x1C0008091: jz   loc_1C0008243             ; null check
0x1C0008097: movzx r8d, cs:word_1C0003022   ; Size = NumberOfBytes (Length+2)
0x1C000809F: xor  edx, edx                   ; Val = 0
0x1C00080A1: mov  rcx, rax                   ; dst = Path
0x1C00080A4: call memset                     ; memset(Path, 0, Length+2) -- ADDED (redundant)
0x1C00080A9: movzx r8d, word ptr [rsi]       ; Size = RegistryPath->Length
0x1C00080AD: mov  rcx, rdi                   ; dst = Path
0x1C00080B0: mov  rdx, [rsi+8]              ; src = RegistryPath->Buffer
0x1C00080B4: call memmove                    ; copy Length bytes
0x1C00080B9: movzx eax, word ptr [rsi]
0x1C00080C1: shr  rax, 1
0x1C00080CC: mov  [rdi+rax*2], r14w          ; wide null-term
0x1C00080D1: call memset                     ; memset(&QueryTable, 0, 0xE0) -- local table

security_findings.entry_point: DriverEntry (0x1C0008008), called once at load by the entry stub GsDriverEntry (0x1C0008260)
call_chain: memmove (0x1C0001B00) copies Length bytes; wide null-terminator write completes the buffer
RtlQueryRegistryValues(RTL_REGISTRY_ABSOLUTE, P, ...) consumes P as a null-terminated path
security_findings.description: The patched build adds memset(Path, 0, NumberOfBytes) at 0x1C00080A4 in DriverEntry, zeroing the full ExAllocatePoolWithTag allocation before the registry path is copied. This is not a security fix. The allocation is Length+2 bytes; the memmove writes bytes [0, Length) and the wide null-terminator write (mov [rdi+rax*2], r14w, rax = Length>>1) writes a 2-byte zero at offset 2*(Length/2). For the even RegistryPath->Length that a valid UNICODE_STRING always has, that offset equals Length and covers [Length, Length+2), so the copy plus terminator initialize the entire allocation in the unpatched build as well. There are therefore no uninitialized pool bytes on the path that runs. Additionally the buffer (global P at 0x1C0003028) is used only as the null-terminated absolute registry path argument to RtlQueryRegistryValues and is never returned to user mode; DriverEntry is init-only, running once at PASSIVE_LEVEL in system-thread context, and is not reachable from an untrusted request surface. The added memset is redundant zero-on-allocate hardening with no reachable behavioral effect.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None. RegistryPath is supplied by the OS at driver load with an even Length; it is not attacker-controllable to an odd length, and the buffer is never surfaced to user mode. No IOCTL, dispatch, WMI, or PnP path in either build reads back the buffer.
security_findings.primitive: None. No information-disclosure or other primitive is demonstrable. For the inputs that occur the unpatched buffer is fully initialized, and it is never exposed to user mode.
security_findings.assembly:
DriverEntry (UNPATCHED) key block:
0x1C0008030: movzx eax, word ptr [rdx]      ; eax = RegistryPath->Length
0x1C0008050: cmp   ax, cx                    ; Length < 0xFFFD ?
0x1C0008053: jnb   loc_1C0008231            ; if >= 0xFFFD, error
0x1C0008059: mov   cs:DriverEntryRegPath, ax ; global 0x1C0003020 = Length
0x1C0008064: add   ax, 2                     ; NumberOfBytes = Length + 2
0x1C0008071: mov   cs:word_1C0003022, dx     ; global 0x1C0003022 = NumberOfBytes
0x1C0008078: call  cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
0x1C0008084: mov   cs:P, rax                 ; global 0x1C0003028 = Path
0x1C000808B: mov   rdi, rax
0x1C000808E: test  rax, rax
0x1C0008091: jz    loc_1C0008231            ; null check
0x1C0008097: movzx r8d, word ptr [rsi]       ; Size = Length
0x1C000809B: mov   rcx, rax
0x1C000809E: mov   rdx, [rsi+8]             ; src = RegistryPath->Buffer
0x1C00080A2: call  memmove                   ; copy Length bytes
0x1C00080A7: movzx eax, word ptr [rsi]
0x1C00080AF: shr   rax, 1
0x1C00080BA: mov   [rdi+rax*2], r14w         ; wide null-term completes the buffer for even Length
0x1C00080BF: call  memset                    ; memset(&QueryTable, 0, 0xE0) -- local RTL_QUERY_REGISTRY_TABLE
0x1C0008188: mov   ecx, 80000000h            ; RTL_REGISTRY_ABSOLUTE
0x1C0008198: call  cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; -> RtlQueryRegistryValues(RTL_REGISTRY_ABSOLUTE, P, ...)
0x1C0008231: mov   eax, 0C000009Ah           ; STATUS_UNRECOGNIZED_VOLUME (error path)

breakpoints.why: Driver initialization routine. Entry with rcx = DriverObject, rdx = RegistryPath (UNICODE_STRING*). At 0x1C0008030, eax = RegistryPath->Length. After ExAllocatePoolWithTag returns at 0x1C0008084, rax = Path. Observe that the unpatched build fully initializes the buffer via memmove (0x1C00080A2) plus the wide null-terminator (0x1C00080BA) for the even Length that occurs.

watch: rsi (preserved from rdx) = RegistryPath UNICODE_STRING*; word [rsi] = Length (even), qword [rsi+8] = Buffer
P (0x1C0003028) = global storing the Path pointer after 0x1C0008084
At 0x1C00080BA: rdi + 2*(Length/2) = null-terminator write position; for even Length this equals Length and covers the last 2 bytes of the allocation
key_offsets: 0x1C0008084: mov cs:P, rax (store buffer pointer in global 0x1C0003028)
0x1C00080BA: wide null-terminator write completes the buffer for even Length
0x1C00080BF: memset(&QueryTable, 0, 0xE0) zeroes the local query table, not Path
0x1C00080A4: added memset(Path, 0, NumberOfBytes) in the PATCHED build (redundant)
0x1C0008198: RtlQueryRegistryValues consumes P as a null-terminated path
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: This code runs once during driver initialization (DriverEntry) at load. RegistryPath is provided by the OS with an even Length and is not attacker-controllable; the buffer is not exposed to user mode. There is no attacker-reachable trigger.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In both builds the registry-path buffer is fully initialized for the even RegistryPath->Length that occurs: unpatched via memmove plus the wide null-terminator, patched additionally via the redundant memset. The buffer is consumed only by RtlQueryRegistryValues as a null-terminated path.
debugging_aids.notes: The buffer is allocated with ExAllocatePoolWithTag (non-zeroing allocator). The added memset is a zero-on-allocate hardening pattern. It has no reachable behavioral effect here because the copy plus wide null-terminator already cover the full allocation for even lengths and the buffer is never returned to user mode. Globals: DriverEntryRegPath (0x1C0003020) = Length, word_1C0003022 (0x1C0003022) = NumberOfBytes, P (0x1C0003028) = Path pointer. Image base is 0x1C0000000.

summary: The only code change between the two irenum.sys builds is in DriverEntry (0x1C0008008): the patched build adds memset(Path, 0, NumberOfBytes) at 0x1C00080A4 to zero the registry-path pool allocation before the path is copied in. This is redundant defensive hardening, not a security fix. For the even RegistryPath->Length that a valid UNICODE_STRING always has, the unpatched build already fully initializes the Length+2 allocation via the memmove (Length bytes) plus the wide null-terminator write at offset Length, leaving no uninitialized bytes. The buffer (global P at 0x1C0003028) is used only as the null-terminated absolute path for RtlQueryRegistryValues and is never returned to user mode, and DriverEntry is init-only and not reachable from an untrusted surface. An independent content-level diff confirms DriverEntry is the only changed function; no security-relevant change was omitted.
